title: Psephellus pecho
taxonomy: Psephellus pecho is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe of the sunflower family, Asteraceae. This plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. It is a perennial herb with leaves that are pinnately divided and a flower head that is yellow and composite. Psephellus pecho produces a number of small, tubular flowers that are arranged in a circular pattern around the center of the head. The plant is often found growing in dry, rocky areas and scrublands.

intro: Psephellus pecho, also known as Serratula pecho, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential in treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Psephellus pecho is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine for its purported benefits in treating digestive issues and as an antiseptic agent.
